Yes. I started working on test-container implementation and will add more error cases post that. The error handling is async which makes it a little tricky to test. Still looking for a good enough way to test it.
Modification to be added here (and maybe in a couple of other files)

On the listener side, the topic name is configurable and the default value is currently set to `cps.cfg-state-events`. I'm not aware of any naming convention for topic names.

https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/cps/cps-temporal/+/121700/3/src/main/resources/application.yml#60

I prefer the property name you had, without referring to kafka, and I'll make the change for `app.listener.data-updated.topic` on temporal.

As it appeared that some effort is required for the design of the feature, a spike has been created: https://jira.onap.org/browse/CPS-474

Then, the implementation may be not only providing a list of dataspaces here.

Method name suggestion to reflect that more complex logic is expected to determine if the notification should be send or not: 

* shouldSendNotification()

Not part of this change but I think that the client should also be able to set the observed timestamp. 

It will require a change on the REST API. To keep this additional information optional for the REST API, if not provided then current timestamp could be set here.
Agree, it would make sense to me.
Don't we need value-serializer and maybe some other kafka configurations for the application also ?
Added the same topic name but used different variable name '${CPS_CHANGE_EVENT_TOPIC:cps.cfg-state-events}'

It should not hang and if it does then it is an issue. 

It keeps checking if consumer has received the message. There might be a case that the consumer may not received message within 60s (timeout) and the test case fails. I don't think it should happen with the timeout of 60seconds, but there is a possibility as the process is async.

Niamh confirmed that the test cases are working fine for her. Hence, I am marking this comment as resolved.

Just need an opinion on it, as i see we are sending notification at the end of each request for cpsdata except get. This looks good case to use interceptors for posthandle.

the docker image for the kafka and postgres test containers are at diff location in the nexus. 

kafka - nexus3.onap.org:10001/confluentinc/cp-kafka:6.1.1
postgres - nexus3.onap.org:10001/library/postgres:13.3

Hence, postgres specific location is defined in the the cps-ri pom.

Sending notification is not an action which needs to be done for all the POST methods. It is required only for DataNode change APIs. And I see this feature more as a part of service layer than controller.

I was initially considering to implement it using 'AOP' or 'event publisher' but both these alternatives are more complex than required for our use case.

try catch block could be inside of if-block. (but if you prefer this way I think it is OK too)

keep scenario/slogans short. Details should be (and are) in the labels below

the problem is that as tests grow and more expectations are added, keeping the test description up to date becomes a nightmare. I prefer if you stick to the recommendation in our conventions and stick to a short 'scenario' without expectation(s) as these are clearly described in the other labels below

The title/slogan (def label) of tests should start with upper case (because thy are not preceded by a word like given,then etc.)
